mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or signal fails to predict direction in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during early-cycle recovery periods are followed by negative 20-day returns in > 50% of observations, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals robust economic activity and consumer spending power, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases are followed by negative 5-day returns in > 55% of cases, or signal loses predictive power (IC < 0.02) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 250, inflationary pressures force monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL > 250 periods yield positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion, reinforcing earnings forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS results in negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or interaction IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, triggering risk aversion and negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines in late-cycle expansions are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03.
